data.content:
Implementation plan

Name and scope
- UI label: Stale Beats for the overview rail; action label: Review stale beats.
- Stale definition for the first implementation: a beat included in the overview surface, not in the terminated overview tab, with `created` more than 7 days before an injected `nowMs`. Use `created` rather than `updated` because the user described backlog age; keep the helper narrow so this can change to last-touched age later.
- Review decisions are advisory and use stable ids: `still_do`, `reshape`, and `drop`. `reshape` may include suggested title/description/acceptance edits, but the first implementation should not mutate workflow state or auto-close beats.

Child hierarchy
- foolery-36d2 Add stale grooming overview rail.
- foolery-6b74 Add stale grooming AI review jobs.
- foolery-3b78 Wire stale grooming controls and status.

Implementation steps
1. Add pure domain helpers in `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming.ts` plus shared types in `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-types.ts`: age cutoff, terminal/overview filtering, sorted stale beat summaries, review decision labels, and request payload shaping. Inject `nowMs` everywhere tests touch time.
2. Add `src/components/stale-beat-grooming-panel.tsx` and keep `src/components/beat-state-overview.tsx` changes small: render a compact right-side rail/header accessory beside the existing Work Items / Exploration / Gates / Terminated tabs, then keep the existing state matrix as the main scrollport. Reuse `AgentDisplayLabel`, existing `Select`, `Input`, `Button`, and existing `onOpenBeat`.
3. Add backend review plumbing parallel to Scope Refinement, but separate: `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-queue.ts`, `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-worker-state.ts`, `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-worker.ts`, `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-job-runner.ts`, and `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-prompt.ts`. Jobs should load each beat from its repo, run the selected registered agent with optional model override, parse tagged JSON, and store per-beat status/result in memory.
4. Add API routes under `src/app/api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews/route.ts`: `GET` returns current statuses/results; `POST` validates explicit selected `agentId`, optional `modelOverride`, and beat targets. Do not silently select an agent. If the selected configured agent is missing, return 400/500 with a distinctive `FOOLERY GROOMING FAILURE` marker and log an ANSI-red banner.
5. Add a small client API module such as `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-api.ts` instead of growing `src/lib/api.ts` past the 500-line limit. Use React Query in the panel to fetch agents/settings and poll review status while jobs are active.
6. If a persistent dispatch setting is desired later, add a `backlogGrooming` action/pool target in settings. For this first slice, prefer the trigger-level agent/model controls the user explicitly allowed; this avoids settings-schema churn and lets users spend leftover model quota per run.

Tests
- `src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming.test.ts`: stale cutoff, invalid/missing dates, terminal/lease exclusion, created-date boundary, payload shaping with repo paths.
- `src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming-prompt.test.ts`: tagged JSON parsing, decision enum validation, update suggestion parsing.
- Route tests modeled on `src/lib/__tests__/refine-scope-route.test.ts`: canonical beat id lookup, invalid/missing agent, explicit model override copied onto the selected agent, enqueue status.
- Component or manual structural tests for overview integration if existing React harness supports it; otherwise keep UI logic pure and cover the payload/status helpers.
- Full gates before integration: `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in the dedicated worktree, then `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build`.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium-large. UI is modest, but the review worker/result store is new infrastructure.
- Main risk: product expectation may mean "last updated more than 7 days ago" rather than "created more than 7 days ago". Current plan chooses created age and isolates that choice.
- Multi-repo overview must process jobs per beat repo path; avoid one giant all-repo prompt.
- Review results are in-memory in the first implementation. Persistent grooming history can be a follow-up if users need results after restart.
- Do not move beats to terminal states or mutate scope automatically from AI output in this first pass.
